### JSON Viewer

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/json-viewer";`

Hand-rolled recursive, collapsible, colorised JSON tree viewer.

Exports: `JsonViewer`, `JsonViewerProps`

Props (`JsonViewerProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional class names applied to the root element. |
| `copyable` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, a CopyButton is shown in the top-right corner. |
| `data` | `unknown` | yes |  | The JSON-serialisable value to display. |
| `defaultExpanded` | `boolean \| number` |  | `1` | Controls how deep the tree is initially expanded. - `true` — expand all nodes. - `false` — collapse all nodes. - `number` — expand nodes whose depth is less than this value (root = 0). |
| `maxHeight` | `number \| string` |  |  | Constrains the viewer height. Accepts any CSS `max-height` value or a pixel number. |
| `rootLabel` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional label rendered at the top-left of the root node. |

Data slots: `json-viewer`

### Leaderboard

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/leaderboard";`

Root of a Leaderboard. A vertical stack of ranked rows, each with a value-proportional fill bar behind its label.

Exports: `Leaderboard`, `LeaderboardAction`, `LeaderboardContent`, `LeaderboardEnd`, `LeaderboardHeader`, `LeaderboardItem`, `LeaderboardItemProps`, `LeaderboardProps`, `LeaderboardStart`, `LeaderboardTitle`

Props (`LeaderboardItemProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"li">, "children">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `\| React.ReactNode \| ((values: LeaderboardItemRenderProps) => React.ReactNode)` |  |  | Row content, or a render-prop receiving `{ value, percentage }`. |
| `maxValue` | `number` |  | `100` | Upper bound of the scale. Defaults to `100`. |
| `minValue` | `number` |  | `0` | Lower bound of the scale. Defaults to `0`. |
| `onAction` | `() => void` |  |  | Makes the row a button and fires on click/Enter/Space. |
| `value` | `number` | yes |  | The row's value, used to size the fill bar. |

Props (`LeaderboardProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

Data slots: `leaderboard`, `leaderboard-action`, `leaderboard-content`, `leaderboard-end`, `leaderboard-header`, `leaderboard-item`, `leaderboard-start`, `leaderboard-title`

### Metric Card

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/metric-card";`

Self-contained metric summary card with an optional delta indicator and sparkline.

Exports: `MetricCard`, `MetricCardDelta`, `MetricCardHeader`, `MetricCardProps`, `MetricCardSparkline`, `MetricCardValue`

Props (`MetricCardProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `title` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Card heading text. |
| `value` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The primary numeric or text value to highlight. |
| `delta` | `{ value: number; label?: string; direction?: "up" \| "down" \| "neutral"; }` |  |  | Optional delta / change indicator. `direction` defaults to `"up"` when `value >= 0` and `"down"` otherwise. Pass `"neutral"` for flat trends. |
| `icon` | `IconSvgElement` |  |  | Trailing icon rendered in the header beside `title`. |
| `description` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Muted description line below the delta. |
| `data` | `number[]` |  |  | Numeric series rendered as a mini sparkline at the bottom of the card. |
| `color` | `string` |  | `"var(--chart-1)"` | Sparkline bar fill color. Defaults to `var(--chart-1)`. |
| `size` | `"default" \| "sm"` |  | `"default"` | Layout size of the card. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes merged onto the root `<Card>` element. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Additional content rendered after the description line, before the sparkline. |

Data slots: `metric-card`, `metric-card-delta`, `metric-card-header`, `metric-card-sparkline`, `metric-card-value`

Heavy dependencies: `recharts`

### QR Code

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/qr-code";`

Root component that renders an SVG QR code and provides context to all child sub-components.

Exports: `QRCode`, `QRCodeDownload`, `QRCodeDownloadProps`, `QRCodeOverlay`, `QRCodeOverlayProps`, `QRCodeProps`, `QRCodeSkeleton`, `QRCodeSkeletonProps`

Props (`QRCodeDownloadProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<typeof Button>`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `filename` | `string` |  | `"qrcode"` | Base filename (without extension) for the downloaded file. |
| `format` | `"svg" \| "png"` |  | `"svg"` | Export format. - `"svg"` — lossless vector, works in all environments. - `"png"` — rasterised at the QR code's native `size`. |

Props (`QRCodeOverlayProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Logo, icon, or any content to display centred over the QR code. |

Props (`QRCodeProps`):
Extends: `Omit< React.ComponentProps<"div">, "children" | "className" | "style" >`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `bgColor` | `string` |  |  | Background colour for the QR code modules. Defaults to the library default (`#ffffff`). |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Nested content — typically QRCodeOverlay and/or QRCodeDownload. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional CSS classes applied to the wrapper `<div>`. |
| `fgColor` | `string` |  |  | Foreground colour for the QR code modules. Defaults to the library default (`#000000`). |
| `level` | `"L" \| "M" \| "Q" \| "H"` |  | `"M"` | Reed–Solomon error-correction level. - `"L"` — 7 % correction (highest data density) - `"M"` — 15 % correction (default) - `"Q"` — 25 % correction - `"H"` — 30 % correction (lowest data density — required when using QRCodeOverlay) |
| `margin` | `number` |  | `0` | Quiet-zone margin expressed in QR *modules* (not pixels). |
| `size` | `number` |  | `160` | Pixel size of the rendered QR code square. |
| `style` | `React.CSSProperties` |  |  | Inline style overrides for the wrapper `<div>`. |
| `title` | `string` |  |  | Accessible name for the QR code `<svg>` (rendered as an SVG `<title>`). A QR code is meaningful content a screen reader cannot otherwise convey, so it always carries an accessible name. |
| `value` | `string` | yes |  | The data to encode into the QR code (e.g. a URL or plain text). |

Props (`QRCodeSkeletonProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `number` |  | `160` | Pixel size of the skeleton square — should match the intended QRCode `size`. |

Data slots: `qr-code`, `qr-code-overlay`, `qr-code-skeleton`

### Radial Gauge

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/radial-gauge";`

Circular radial-bar gauge that fills proportionally to a `value / max` ratio.

Exports: `RadialGauge`, `RadialGaugeProps`, `RadialGaugeThresholds`

Props (`RadialGaugeProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `number` | yes |  | Current value to display. |
| `max` | `number` |  | `100` | Maximum possible value. Defaults to `100`. |
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional label rendered beneath the center percentage. |
| `centerLabel` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Replaces the default `"N%"` text rendered in the center of the gauge. |
| `color` | `string` |  |  | Explicit fill color; overrides threshold-based color selection. |
| `thresholds` | `RadialGaugeThresholds` |  |  | Thresholds used to pick warning/danger colors automatically. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional class names merged onto the outermost gauge wrapper div. |

Data slots: `radial-gauge`

Heavy dependencies: `recharts`

### Relative Time Card

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/relative-time-card";`

Shows a compact, formatted timestamp that — on hover — reveals a card with the live relative time and the same instant rendered across one or more timezones (plus the viewer's local zone).

Exports: `RelativeTimeCard`, `RelativeTimeCardProps`

Props (`RelativeTimeCardProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"button">, "children">`, `VariantProps<typeof triggerVariants>`, `Pick< React.ComponentProps<typeof HoverCard>, "open" | "defaultOpen" | "onOpenChange" >`, `Pick<React.ComponentProps<typeof HoverCardTrigger>, "delay" | "closeDelay">`, `Pick< React.ComponentProps<typeof HoverCardContent>, "side" | "align" | "sideOffset" | "alignOffset" >`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `date` | `Date \| string \| number` | yes |  | The date to display. Accepts a `Date`, an ISO string, or a numeric epoch timestamp — all are normalised via `new Date(date)`. |
| `timezones` | `string[]` |  | `["UTC"]` | IANA time-zone identifiers shown as rows in the card. The viewer's local timezone is always appended as a final row. |
| `updateInterval` | `number` |  | `1000` | How often (ms) the relative-time string in the card refreshes. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Custom trigger label. When omitted, the trigger shows the formatted absolute timestamp (e.g. "Jun 1, 2024, 12:00 PM"). |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `muted`, `ghost` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `relative-time-card-content`, `relative-time-card-trigger`

### Sortable

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/sortable";`

Drag-to-reorder container built on dnd-kit. Wraps its children in a `DndContext` + `SortableContext` and renders a floating overlay that mirrors the actively dragged SortableItem.

Exports: `Sortable`, `SortableItem`, `SortableItemHandle`, `SortableItemHandleProps`, `SortableItemProps`, `SortableProps`

Props (`SortableItemHandleProps`):
Extends: `ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `cursor` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `true` (default), applies `cursor-grab` / `cursor-grabbing` to signal draggability. Set `false` to manage the cursor yourself. |

Props (`SortableItemProps`):
Extends: `ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, the item cannot be dragged and is dimmed. |
| `value` | `string` | yes |  | Stable id for this item; must equal `getItemValue(item)` on the parent Sortable. |

Props (`SortableProps`):
Extends: `Omit<ComponentProps<"div">, "onDragStart" | "onDragEnd">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `getItemValue` | `(item: T) => string` | yes |  | Derives the stable string id for an item. Must match the `value` passed to the corresponding SortableItem. |
| `modifiers` | `Modifiers` |  |  | dnd-kit modifiers applied to both the drag and the overlay. |
| `onDragEnd` | `(event: DragEndEvent) => void` |  |  | Forwarded to dnd-kit's `DndContext` `onDragEnd` (fires before reorder). |
| `onDragStart` | `(event: DragStartEvent) => void` |  |  | Forwarded to dnd-kit's `DndContext` `onDragStart`. |
| `onMove` | `(event: { event: DragEndEvent; activeIndex: number; overIndex: number; }) => void` |  |  | Escape hatch invoked instead of the built-in `arrayMove`. Receives the raw drag event plus the resolved source/target indices, letting you run a custom reorder (e.g. server-synced). When provided, `onValueChange` is **not** called automatically. |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: T[]) => void` | yes |  | Called with the reordered list after a successful drag. |
| `strategy` | `SortableStrategy` |  | `"vertical"` | Layout strategy used for measuring/reflowing during a drag. `"vertical"` (default) for stacked lists, `"horizontal"` for rows, and `"grid"` for wrapped two-dimensional grids. |
| `value` | `T[]` | yes |  | The ordered list of items rendered as SortableItems. |

Data slots: `sortable`, `sortable-item`, `sortable-item-handle`

### Status Badge

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/status-badge";`

A Badge that maps an orchestration status keyword to a consistent colour, a leading status dot, and a humanised label.

Exports: `DEFAULT_STATUS_CONFIG`, `STATUS_CONFIG`, `StatusBadge`, `StatusBadgeProps`, `StatusConfig`, `formatStatusLabel`, `getStatusConfig`

Props (`StatusBadgeProps`):
Extends: `Omit<BadgeProps, "children">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `string` | yes |  | The status keyword, e.g. `"running"`, `"failed"`, `"timed_out"`. |
| `showDot` | `boolean` |  | `true` | Show the leading coloured dot. Defaults to `true`. |
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Override the rendered text; defaults to a humanised `status`. |
| `pulse` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, renders an `animate-ping` ring behind the dot to signal a "live" or real-time state. |
| `dotOnly` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, renders only the dot with no visible text label. |

Data slots: `status-badge`, `status-badge-dot`

### Table

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/table";`

Responsive data table with a horizontally scrollable container.

Exports: `Table`, `TableBody`, `TableCaption`, `TableCell`, `TableFooter`, `TableHead`, `TableHeader`, `TableProps`, `TableRow`, `tableVariants`

Props (`TableProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"table">`, `TableVariantProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default" \| "lg"` |  | `"default"` | Controls cell height and padding for all TableHead and TableCell elements via a `data-size` attribute on the container div. - `sm` — `h-6 px-1.5 text-xs` (compact) - `default` — `h-7 px-2 text-sm` (unchanged) - `lg` — `h-9 px-3 text-sm` (spacious) |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `striped`, `bordered` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `table`, `table-body`, `table-caption`, `table-cell`, `table-container`, `table-footer`, `table-head`, `table-header`, `table-row`

### Tag Group

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/tag-group";`

An accessible group container for a set of selectable or removable tags.

Exports: `Tag`, `TagGroup`, `TagGroupProps`, `TagList`, `TagListProps`, `TagProps`, `tagVariants`

Props (`TagGroupProps`):
Extends: `TagGroupPropsRac`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional Tailwind classes merged onto the wrapper `<div>`. |
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional label text or element rendered above the tag list. |

Props (`TagListProps`):
Extends: `TagListPropsRac<T>`

Props (`TagProps`):
Extends: `TagPropsRac`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional Tailwind classes merged onto the tag element. |
| `variant` | `VariantProps<typeof tagVariants>["variant"]` |  |  | Visual style variant. Defaults to `"default"`. |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `secondary`, `outline` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `tag`, `tag-group`, `tag-list`

### Timeline

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/timeline";`

Stepped progress tracker that renders a sequence of labeled events.

Exports: `Timeline`, `TimelineContent`, `TimelineContextValue`, `TimelineDate`, `TimelineHeader`, `TimelineIndicator`, `TimelineIndicatorProps`, `TimelineIntent`, `TimelineItem`, `TimelineItemProps`, `TimelineProps`, `TimelineSeparator`, `TimelineTitle`

Props (`TimelineIndicatorProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `IconSvgElement` |  |  | Optional Hugeicons icon rendered centered inside the indicator bubble. When present, the indicator automatically becomes a flex-center container. Takes precedence over `children` in render order (rendered first). |

Props (`TimelineItemProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `step` | `number` | yes |  | 1-based index of this step; compared against the active step to set `data-completed`. |

Props (`TimelineProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `defaultValue` | `number` |  | `1` | Initial active step index (uncontrolled). Defaults to `1`. |
| `value` | `number` |  |  | Active step index (controlled). |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: number) => void` |  |  | Called when the active step changes. |
| `orientation` | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` |  | `"vertical"` | Direction of the step track. Defaults to `"vertical"`. |
| `variant` | `"solid" \| "dotted"` |  | `"solid"` | Connector line rendering style. - `"solid"` (default) — filled bar using `bg-*` classes. - `"dotted"` — dashed border line drawn via `border-*` + `border-dashed`. |
| `intent` | `TimelineIntent` |  | `"primary"` | Colour intent applied to completed-step indicators and separators. Defaults to `"primary"`. |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default"` |  | `"default"` | Size preset for indicators, gaps, and text. - `"default"` — existing proportions (no change). - `"sm"` — smaller indicator (`size-4`), tighter gaps, smaller text. |

Data slots: `timeline`, `timeline-content`, `timeline-header`, `timeline-indicator`, `timeline-item`, `timeline-separator`, `timeline-title`

### Tracker

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/tracker";`

A compact status timeline — a row of small colored blocks, each with an optional hover tooltip. Ideal for uptime, build history, or any per-period status strip (à la a status-page incident bar).

Exports: `Tracker`, `TrackerBlockProps`, `TrackerProps`

Props (`TrackerBlockProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `color` | `string` |  |  | Tailwind background class for this block (e.g. `"bg-success"`). |
| `defaultBackgroundColor` | `string` |  |  | Background class used when `color` is omitted. |
| `disabledTooltip` | `boolean` |  |  | Skip the tooltip entirely for this block. |
| `key` | `string \| number` |  |  | Stable React key when the data has no natural id. |
| `tooltip` | `string` |  |  | Tooltip label shown on hover/focus. |

Props (`TrackerProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`, `Pick<TrackerBlockProps, "disabledTooltip">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | `TrackerBlockProps[]` | yes | `[]` | Ordered list of blocks to render left-to-right. |
| `defaultBackgroundColor` | `string` |  |  | Background class for blocks that omit their own `color`. |

Data slots: `tracker`, `tracker-block`

### Tree

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/tree";`

A hierarchical tree view for displaying nested data with support for keyboard navigation, single/multiple selection, and expand/collapse.

Exports: `Tree`, `TreeItem`, `TreeItemProps`, `TreeProps`

Props (`TreeItemProps`):
Extends: `Omit<TreeItemPropsRac<T>, "textValue" | "children">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Nested `<TreeItem>` elements that form the item's children. |
| `icon` | `IconSvgElement` |  |  | Optional leading icon rendered before the label. |
| `textValue` | `string` |  |  | Typeahead / accessibility string for the row. |
| `title` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The visible label for the row. |

Data slots: `tree`, `tree-item`

## Navigation

### Breadcrumb

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/breadcrumb";`

Accessible navigation landmark that shows the user's location in the site hierarchy.

Exports: `Breadcrumb`, `BreadcrumbEllipsis`, `BreadcrumbItem`, `BreadcrumbLink`, `BreadcrumbList`, `BreadcrumbPage`, `BreadcrumbProps`, `BreadcrumbSeparator`

Props (`BreadcrumbProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"nav">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `BreadcrumbSize` |  | `"default"` | Controls text size and gap across all descendant breadcrumb parts. |
| `separator` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Default separator node rendered between every BreadcrumbItem when the caller omits explicit `<BreadcrumbSeparator>` elements. Forwarded via React context so each `BreadcrumbSeparator` can fall back to it. |

Data slots: `breadcrumb`, `breadcrumb-ellipsis`, `breadcrumb-item`, `breadcrumb-list`, `breadcrumb-page`, `breadcrumb-separator`

### Command

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/command";`

A keyboard-navigable command palette built on the `cmdk` primitive.

Exports: `Command`, `CommandDialog`, `CommandEmpty`, `CommandGroup`, `CommandInput`, `CommandItem`, `CommandList`, `CommandListSize`, `CommandProps`, `CommandSeparator`, `CommandShortcut`

Props (`CommandProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<typeof CommandPrimitive>`

Data slots: `command`, `command-empty`, `command-group`, `command-input`, `command-input-wrapper`, `command-item`, `command-list`, `command-separator`, `command-shortcut`

### Command Menu

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/command-menu";`

A ⌘K command palette that combines the design-system CommandDialog with a global hotkey from `react-hotkeys-hook`.

Exports: `CommandMenu`, `CommandMenuGroup`, `CommandMenuItem`, `CommandMenuProps`

Props (`CommandMenuProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `groups` | `CommandMenuGroup[]` | yes |  | Groups of items rendered inside the palette. |
| `open` | `boolean` |  |  | Controlled open state; when provided alongside `onOpenChange` the parent fully owns open/close. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` |  |  | Called when the palette requests an open-state change. |
| `placeholder` | `string` |  | `"Type a command or search..."` | Placeholder text shown in the search input. |
| `hotkey` | `string` |  | `"mod+k"` | Global hotkey that toggles the palette. Uses `react-hotkeys-hook` notation — `"mod"` maps to `⌘` on macOS and `Ctrl` on Windows/Linux. |
| `trigger` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional element rendered outside the dialog; clicking it opens the palette. Typically a `<Button>` or an `<kbd>` hint. |
| `emptyMessage` | `string` |  | `"No results found."` | Message shown by CommandEmpty when no items match the current query. |

Data slots: `command-menu`, `command-menu-trigger`

### Menubar

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/menubar";`

A horizontal bar of labeled menu triggers, each opening a dropdown panel.

Exports: `Menubar`, `MenubarCheckboxItem`, `MenubarContent`, `MenubarGroup`, `MenubarItem`, `MenubarLabel`, `MenubarMenu`, `MenubarPortal`, `MenubarProps`, `MenubarRadioGroup`, `MenubarRadioItem`, `MenubarSeparator`, `MenubarShortcut`, `MenubarSub`, `MenubarSubContent`, `MenubarSubTrigger`, `MenubarTrigger`

Props (`MenubarProps`):
Extends: `MenubarPrimitive.Props`

Data slots: `menubar`, `menubar-checkbox-item`, `menubar-content`, `menubar-group`, `menubar-item`, `menubar-label`, `menubar-menu`, `menubar-portal`, `menubar-radio-group`, `menubar-radio-item`, `menubar-separator`, `menubar-shortcut`, `menubar-sub`, `menubar-sub-content`, `menubar-sub-trigger`, `menubar-trigger`

### NavigationMenu

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/navigation-menu";`

Horizontal navigation bar with flyout content panels.

Exports: `NavigationMenu`, `NavigationMenuContent`, `NavigationMenuIndicator`, `NavigationMenuItem`, `NavigationMenuLink`, `NavigationMenuList`, `NavigationMenuPositioner`, `NavigationMenuProps`, `NavigationMenuTrigger`, `navigationMenuTriggerStyle`

Props (`NavigationMenuProps`):
Extends: `NavigationMenuPrimitive.Root.Props`, `Pick<NavigationMenuPrimitive.Positioner.Props, "align">`

Data slots: `navigation-menu`, `navigation-menu-content`, `navigation-menu-indicator`, `navigation-menu-item`, `navigation-menu-link`, `navigation-menu-list`, `navigation-menu-trigger`

### NavigationRail

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/navigation-rail";`

A fixed-width vertical nav column that holds icon-only navigation items.

Exports: `NavigationRail`, `NavigationRailFooter`, `NavigationRailFooterProps`, `NavigationRailHeader`, `NavigationRailHeaderProps`, `NavigationRailItem`, `NavigationRailItemProps`, `NavigationRailProps`, `NavigationRailSection`, `NavigationRailSectionProps`

Props (`NavigationRailFooterProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Content rendered inside the footer (typically a profile item). |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional class names merged onto the footer wrapper. |

Props (`NavigationRailHeaderProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Content rendered inside the header (typically a logo or app icon). |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional class names merged onto the header wrapper. |

Props (`NavigationRailItemProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `IconSvgElement` | yes |  | Hugeicons SVG element rendered as the button icon. |
| `label` | `string` | yes |  | Accessible text shown in the tooltip and as `sr-only` content. |
| `href` | `string` |  |  | Optional URL; pass when the item should behave as an anchor. |
| `onClick` | `() => void` |  |  | Click handler for the icon button. |
| `active` | `boolean` |  |  | Marks the item as the currently active route. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  |  | Disables pointer events and reduces opacity when `true`. |
| `badge` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional indicator node (e.g. `<Ping>`) absolutely positioned at the top-right corner of the button. |

Props (`NavigationRailProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `orientation` | `"left" \| "right"` |  | `"left"` | Side of the layout the rail is anchored to; flips border and order. |

Props (`NavigationRailSectionProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | NavigationRailItem elements to render in this group. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional class names merged onto the section wrapper. |

Data slots: `navigation-rail`, `navigation-rail-footer`, `navigation-rail-header`, `navigation-rail-item`, `navigation-rail-section`

### Pagination

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/pagination";`

Accessible navigation landmark for moving between pages of content.

Exports: `Pagination`, `PaginationContent`, `PaginationEllipsis`, `PaginationItem`, `PaginationLink`, `PaginationNext`, `PaginationPrevious`, `PaginationProps`

Props (`PaginationProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"nav">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `size` | `PaginationSize` |  | `"default"` | Scales all page-button controls uniformly. |

Data slots: `pagination`, `pagination-content`, `pagination-ellipsis`, `pagination-item`, `pagination-link`

### Scrollspy

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/scrollspy";`

Tracks the section currently in view and marks its corresponding navigation anchor with `data-active="true"`. Anchors opt in via `data-{dataAttribute}-anchor="<section-id>"`; clicking one smooth-scrolls to its section and (optionally) updates the URL hash.

Exports: `Scrollspy`, `ScrollspyProps`

Props (`ScrollspyProps`):
Extends: `Omit<ComponentProps<"div">, "children">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `ReactNode` | yes |  | The navigation links to track; anchors carry `data-{dataAttribute}-anchor`. |
| `dataAttribute` | `string` |  | `"scrollspy"` | The data-attribute namespace used to find anchors and apply offsets, e.g. `"scrollspy"` looks for `data-scrollspy-anchor`. Defaults to `"scrollspy"`. |
| `history` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `true` (default), the active section id is synced to the URL hash. |
| `offset` | `number` |  | `0` | Pixels subtracted from each section's offset before comparing against the scroll position. Override per anchor with `data-{dataAttribute}-offset`. |
| `onUpdate` | `(id: string) => void` |  |  | Called with the active section id whenever the active anchor changes. |
| `smooth` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `true` (default), click-to-scroll uses smooth scrolling. |
| `targetRef` | `RefObject<HTMLElement \| Document \| null \| undefined>` |  |  | The scroll container to observe. Defaults to the document/window when omitted. Pass a ref to a scrollable element (or one wrapping a `data-slot="scroll-area-viewport"`) to spy inside it. |

Data slots: `scroll-area-viewport`, `scrollspy`

### Sidebar

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/sidebar";`

The sidebar panel itself — a collapsible column rendered inside a SidebarProvider.

Exports: `Sidebar`, `SidebarCard`, `SidebarCardContent`, `SidebarCardDescription`, `SidebarCardFooter`, `SidebarCardHeader`, `SidebarCardProps`, `SidebarCardTitle`, `SidebarContent`, `SidebarFooter`, `SidebarGroup`, `SidebarGroupAction`, `SidebarGroupContent`, `SidebarGroupLabel`, `SidebarGroupProps`, `SidebarHeader`, `SidebarInput`, `SidebarInset`, `SidebarMenu`, `SidebarMenuAction`, `SidebarMenuBadge`, `SidebarMenuButton`, `SidebarMenuButtonProps`, `SidebarMenuDragHandle`, `SidebarMenuFlyout`, `SidebarMenuItem`, `SidebarMenuItemProps`, `SidebarMenuProps`, `SidebarMenuSkeleton`, `SidebarMenuSub`, `SidebarMenuSubButton`, `SidebarMenuSubItem`, `SidebarMenuSubProps`, `SidebarPanel`, `SidebarPanelProps`, `SidebarProps`, `SidebarProvider`, `SidebarProviderProps`, `SidebarRail`, `SidebarRailButton`, `SidebarRailButtonProps`, `SidebarSearchButton`, `SidebarSearchButtonProps`, `SidebarSeparator`, `SidebarSwitcher`, `SidebarSwitcherCurrent`, `SidebarSwitcherItem`, `SidebarSwitcherItemProps`, `SidebarSwitcherProps`, `SidebarToggleRail`, `SidebarTrigger`, `SidebarUserButton`, `SidebarUserButtonProps`, `useSidebar`

Props (`SidebarCardProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `showOnCollapse` | `boolean` |  | `false` | Show the card when the sidebar is collapsed to icon width. Defaults to `false` — most upgrade / onboarding prompts only make sense when there's room for the headline copy. |

Props (`SidebarGroupProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `collapsible` | `string` |  |  | Persistence key for the group's open/closed state. When set, the group becomes a Base UI `Collapsible` and its SidebarGroupLabel promotes itself to a disclosure trigger with a rotating chevron. |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` |  | `true` | Initial open state when uncontrolled. Defaults to `true`. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` |  |  | Called when the group's open state changes in controlled mode. |
| `open` | `boolean` |  |  | Controlled openness; pair with onOpenChange. |
| `pinned` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, the group floats to the bottom of SidebarContent via `margin-top: auto` — handy for "Settings" / "Help" rows that should always sit at the foot of the nav list. |

Props (`SidebarMenuButtonProps`):
Extends: `useRender.ComponentProps<"button">`, `React.ComponentProps<"button">`, `VariantProps<typeof sidebarMenuButtonVariants>`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `active` | `boolean` |  | `false` | Whether this row represents the current page. Sets `data-active` and `aria-current="page"`. |
| `tooltip` | `string \| React.ComponentProps<typeof TooltipContent>` |  |  | Tooltip shown when the sidebar is collapsed to icon or when the label is truncated. |
| `subMenu` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, promotes this button into a Base UI `Collapsible.Trigger` for the enclosing SidebarMenuItem's sub-menu and appends a rotating chevron at the trailing edge. |

Props (`SidebarMenuItemProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"li">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `string` |  |  | Persistence key shared by this item's hasSubMenu button and its SidebarMenuSub. Required when the item contains a sub-menu; otherwise leave unset and the item behaves like a plain `<li>`. |

Props (`SidebarMenuProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"ul">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `items` | `string[]` |  |  | Ordered list of stable item ids. Required when `reorderable`. |
| `onReorder` | `(items: string[]) => void` |  |  | Fired with the reshuffled list after a successful drag. |
| `reorderable` | `boolean` |  |  | Opt into drag-to-reorder. When `true`, pair with `items` + `onReorder` and give each child SidebarMenuItem a `value` matching one of the `items` entries. The menu renders as a `role="list"` div so the dnd-kit wrappers can sit inside without breaking HTML semantics. |

Props (`SidebarMenuSubProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"ul">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `string` | yes |  | Persistence key — must match the enclosing SidebarMenuItem's `value`. Required: the sub-menu cannot animate open/closed without it. |

Props (`SidebarPanelProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `string` | yes |  | Stable id matched against `activeRailItem` to decide visibility. |

Props (`SidebarProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `side` | `"left" \| "right"` |  | `"left"` | Viewport edge the panel anchors to; `"left"` (default) or `"right"`. |
| `variant` | `"sidebar" \| "floating" \| "inset"` |  | `"sidebar"` | Visual treatment of the sidebar surface. - `"sidebar"` — flat panel flush with the viewport edge (default). - `"floating"` — rounded corners and a shadow ring. - `"inset"` — makes the content area (SidebarInset) appear nested inside the sidebar colour. |
| `collapsible` | `"offcanvas" \| "icon" \| "rail" \| "none"` |  | `"offcanvas"` | Collapse mode when the sidebar is toggled closed. - `"offcanvas"` — slides the panel fully off-screen (default). - `"icon"` — shrinks to icon width, hiding labels. - `"rail"` — dual-panel mode: a narrow icon column (SidebarRail) stays fixed while a wider secondary panel (SidebarPanel) slides in / out based on which SidebarRailButton is active. - `"none"` — disables collapsing; the panel is always visible. |

Props (`SidebarProviderProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` |  | `true` | Initial open state when uncontrolled; defaults to `true`. |
| `open` | `boolean` |  |  | Controlled open state; pair with onOpenChange. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` |  |  | Called when the sidebar's open state changes in controlled mode. |

Props (`SidebarRailButtonProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"button">, "title" | "value">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Icon node rendered as the button's content. |
| `tooltip` | `string` |  |  | Optional accessible name / hover tooltip. |
| `value` | `string` | yes |  | Stable id selecting which SidebarPanel this button opens. |

Props (`SidebarSearchButtonProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"button">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `onTrigger` | `() => void` |  |  | Fires on click and on the registered keyboard shortcut. |
| `placeholder` | `string` |  | `"Search…"` | Placeholder text shown in the row. Defaults to `"Search…"`. |
| `shortcut` | `string \| null` |  | `"⌘K"` | Keyboard hint glyph displayed at the trailing edge (e.g. `"⌘K"`). Pass `null` to hide the hint while still wiring the shortcut handler. |
| `shortcutKey` | `string` |  | `"k"` | Character key that triggers the shortcut combo with Cmd/Ctrl. Defaults to `"k"`. |

Props (`SidebarSwitcherItemProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"button">, "title">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `logo` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional logo / avatar node rendered before the name. |
| `meta` | `string` |  |  | Optional secondary metadata line (e.g. plan, role). Rendered underneath the name in muted text. |
| `name` | `string` | yes |  | Display name of the workspace. |
| `selected` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, the item is rendered as the active selection and shows a trailing check glyph. Surfaces as `data-selected="true"` on the DOM node for styling hooks. |

Props (`SidebarSwitcherProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"button">, "title">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Popover body — typically a list of SidebarSwitcherItems plus any auxiliary actions (e.g. "Create workspace"). |
| `current` | `SidebarSwitcherCurrent` | yes |  | The currently selected workspace; controls the trigger row. |

Props (`SidebarUserButtonProps`):
Extends: `Omit<React.ComponentProps<"button">, "title">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `avatar` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Avatar element rendered to the leading edge (typically an `<Avatar>` or `<img>`). Sized down to fit the row at `size-7`. |
| `email` | `string` |  |  | Optional secondary line — often the user's email. |
| `menu` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Menu body — Base UI `MenuPrimitive.Item` and friends. When omitted, the button still renders the chevron unless static is set; clicks fall through to the consumer's `onClick`. |
| `name` | `string` | yes |  | Primary display name. |
| `static` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, the row is a plain account chip with no menu wrapper and no chevron — handy when the surrounding shell already provides the account dropdown elsewhere. |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `outline` (default: `default`)
- `size`: `default`, `sm`, `lg` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `sidebar`, `sidebar-card`, `sidebar-card-content`, `sidebar-card-description`, `sidebar-card-footer`, `sidebar-card-header`, `sidebar-card-title`, `sidebar-container`, `sidebar-content`, `sidebar-footer`, `sidebar-gap`, `sidebar-group`, `sidebar-group-content`, `sidebar-group-label-chevron`, `sidebar-header`, `sidebar-inner`, `sidebar-input`, `sidebar-inset`, `sidebar-menu`, `sidebar-menu-badge`, `sidebar-menu-button-chevron`, `sidebar-menu-drag-handle`, `sidebar-menu-flyout`, `sidebar-menu-item`, `sidebar-menu-skeleton`, `sidebar-menu-sub`, `sidebar-menu-sub-item`, `sidebar-menu-sub-panel`, `sidebar-panel`, `sidebar-rail`, `sidebar-rail-button`, `sidebar-rail-column`, `sidebar-rail-panel-wrap`, `sidebar-search-button`, `sidebar-separator`, `sidebar-switcher`, `sidebar-switcher-body`, `sidebar-switcher-chevron`, `sidebar-switcher-item`, `sidebar-switcher-item-check`, `sidebar-switcher-item-logo`, `sidebar-switcher-logo`, `sidebar-switcher-popover`, `sidebar-toggle-rail`, `sidebar-toggle-rail-glyph`, `sidebar-trigger`, `sidebar-user-button`, `sidebar-user-button-avatar`, `sidebar-user-button-body`, `sidebar-user-button-chevron`, `sidebar-user-button-menu`, `sidebar-wrapper`

### Stepper

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/stepper";`

A multi-step progress indicator that tracks which step is currently active.

Exports: `Stepper`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperItem`, `StepperProps`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperTrigger`

Props (`StepperProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `defaultValue` | `number` |  | `0` | Initial active step index used in uncontrolled mode; ignored when value is provided. |
| `value` | `number` |  |  | Controlled active step index; when set the component skips internal state and defers all updates to `onValueChange`. |
| `onValueChange` | `(value: number) => void` |  |  | Called with the new step index whenever a StepperTrigger is clicked or `setActiveStep` is invoked programmatically. |
| `orientation` | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` |  | `"horizontal"` | Layout direction; cascades to all sub-components via `data-orientation`. |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default"` |  | `"default"` | Scaling preset that controls indicator size and label text. - `"default"` — `size-8` indicator, `text-sm` labels (existing behaviour). - `"sm"` — `size-6` indicator, `text-xs` labels. |
| `compact` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, applies tighter gap between items and a shorter connector (`h-8` vertical / `h-px` horizontal) for space-constrained layouts. |

Data slots: `stepper`, `stepper-description`, `stepper-indicator`, `stepper-item`, `stepper-separator`, `stepper-title`

### Tabs

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/tabs";`

Layered content panel controlled by a row (or column) of tab buttons.

Exports: `Tabs`, `TabsContent`, `TabsList`, `TabsProps`, `TabsTrigger`, `tabsListVariants`

Props (`TabsProps`):
Extends: `TabsPrimitive.Root.Props`

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `line`, `underline` (default: `default`)
- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `tabs`, `tabs-content`, `tabs-list`, `tabs-trigger`

## Overlays

### Alert Dialog

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/alert-dialog";`

Blocking confirmation dialog that requires the user to make a choice before continuing.

Exports: `AlertDialog`, `AlertDialogAction`, `AlertDialogCancel`, `AlertDialogContent`, `AlertDialogDescription`, `AlertDialogFooter`, `AlertDialogHeader`, `AlertDialogMedia`, `AlertDialogOverlay`, `AlertDialogPortal`, `AlertDialogProps`, `AlertDialogTitle`, `AlertDialogTrigger`, `alertDialogContentVariants`

Props (`AlertDialogProps`):
Extends: `AlertDialogPrimitive.Root.Props`

Variants:
- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg`, `xl`, `full` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `alert-dialog`, `alert-dialog-action`, `alert-dialog-cancel`, `alert-dialog-content`, `alert-dialog-description`, `alert-dialog-footer`, `alert-dialog-header`, `alert-dialog-media`, `alert-dialog-overlay`, `alert-dialog-portal`, `alert-dialog-title`, `alert-dialog-trigger`

### Context Menu

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/context-menu";`

Right-click (or long-press) contextual menu anchored to an arbitrary region of the page.

Exports: `ContextMenu`, `ContextMenuCheckboxItem`, `ContextMenuContent`, `ContextMenuGroup`, `ContextMenuItem`, `ContextMenuLabel`, `ContextMenuPortal`, `ContextMenuProps`, `ContextMenuRadioGroup`, `ContextMenuRadioItem`, `ContextMenuSeparator`, `ContextMenuShortcut`, `ContextMenuSub`, `ContextMenuSubContent`, `ContextMenuSubTrigger`, `ContextMenuTrigger`

Props (`ContextMenuProps`):
Extends: `ContextMenuPrimitive.Root.Props`

Data slots: `context-menu`, `context-menu-checkbox-item`, `context-menu-content`, `context-menu-group`, `context-menu-item`, `context-menu-label`, `context-menu-portal`, `context-menu-radio-group`, `context-menu-radio-item`, `context-menu-separator`, `context-menu-shortcut`, `context-menu-sub`, `context-menu-sub-content`, `context-menu-sub-trigger`, `context-menu-trigger`

### Detail Sheet

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/detail-sheet";`

A right-anchored (by default) detail / inspection panel built as a structured recipe over the design-system Sheet.

Exports: `DetailSheet`, `DetailSheetProps`, `DetailSheetRow`, `DetailSheetRowProps`, `DetailSheetSection`, `DetailSheetSectionProps`

Props (`DetailSheetProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `open` | `boolean` | yes |  | Whether the sheet is currently open. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | yes |  | Callback invoked whenever the sheet requests an open-state change. The first argument is the requested `open` value; forward it to your state setter to keep the sheet controlled. |
| `title` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Main heading rendered in a SheetTitle. |
| `meta` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional muted subtitle rendered beneath the title (e.g. a record ID or status chip). Mapped to `SheetDescription` so it doubles as the accessible description for screen readers. |
| `footer` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional sticky footer rendered via SheetFooter. Typically holds a primary action and a cancel/close control. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The scrollable body of the sheet. Typically composed of DetailSheetSection blocks each containing DetailSheetRow pairs. |
| `side` | `"left" \| "right" \| "top" \| "bottom"` |  | `"right"` | Which screen edge the sheet slides in from. Forwarded directly to `SheetContent`'s `side` prop. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the `SheetContent` element. |

Props (`DetailSheetRowProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Muted descriptor rendered on the left side of the row. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The value displayed on the right side, wrapped in `font-mono text-xs` styling by default. Pass a pre-styled node to override the wrapper. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the row's `flex` wrapper `<div>`. |

Props (`DetailSheetSectionProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `heading` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Section heading rendered as an uppercase muted `<h4>`. Keep it short — one or two words. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The section body; typically one or more DetailSheetRows. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the `<section>` element. |

Data slots: `detail-sheet`, `detail-sheet-row`, `detail-sheet-section`

### Dialog

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/dialog";`

Modal overlay that interrupts the user to focus on a single task or message.

Props (`DialogProps`):
Extends: `DialogPrimitive.Root.Props`

Variants:
- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg`, `xl`, `full` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `dialog`, `dialog-close`, `dialog-content`, `dialog-description`, `dialog-footer`, `dialog-header`, `dialog-overlay`, `dialog-portal`, `dialog-title`, `dialog-trigger`

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/drawer";`

Heavy dependencies: `vaul`

### Dropdown Menu

Extends: `MenuPrimitive.Root.Props`

Data slots: `dropdown-menu`, `dropdown-menu-checkbox-item`, `dropdown-menu-checkbox-item-indicator`, `dropdown-menu-content`, `dropdown-menu-group`, `dropdown-menu-item`, `dropdown-menu-label`, `dropdown-menu-portal`, `dropdown-menu-radio-group`, `dropdown-menu-radio-item`, `dropdown-menu-radio-item-indicator`, `dropdown-menu-separator`, `dropdown-menu-shortcut`, `dropdown-menu-sub`, `dropdown-menu-sub-content`, `dropdown-menu-sub-trigger`, `dropdown-menu-trigger`

Extends: `PopoverPrimitive.Root.Props`

### Sheet

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/sheet";`

### Tooltip

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/tooltip";`

Short, non-interactive label that appears near an element on hover or focus, built on Base UI's `Tooltip` primitive.

Exports: `Tooltip`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipContentProps`, `TooltipProps`, `TooltipProvider`, `TooltipTrigger`, `tooltipContentVariants`

Props (`TooltipContentProps`):
Extends: `TooltipPrimitive.Popup.Props`, `Pick< TooltipPrimitive.Positioner.Props, "align" | "alignOffset" | "side" | "sideOffset" >`, `VariantProps<typeof tooltipContentVariants>`

Props (`TooltipProps`):
Extends: `TooltipPrimitive.Root.Props`

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `light` (default: `default`)
- `size`: `sm`, `default` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `kbd`, `tooltip`, `tooltip-content`, `tooltip-provider`, `tooltip-trigger`

## Feedback

### Alert

Props (`AlertProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`, `VariantProps<typeof alertVariants>`

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `info`, `success`, `warning`, `destructive`, `invert` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `alert`, `alert-action`, `alert-description`, `alert-title`

### Banner

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/banner";`

Root container for the composable Banner.

Exports: `Banner`, `BannerActions`, `BannerClose`, `BannerContent`, `BannerDescription`, `BannerIcon`, `BannerProps`, `BannerTitle`, `NoticeBanner`, `NoticeBannerAction`, `NoticeBannerVariants`, `bannerVariants`, `noticeBannerVariants`

Props (`BannerProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`, `VariantProps<typeof bannerVariants>`

Variants:
- `variant`: `info`, `success`, `warning`, `destructive` (default: `info`)
- `layout`: `inline`, `full-width` (default: `inline`)
- `size`: `sm`, `default` (default: `default`)

### Chart Empty State

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/chart-empty-state";`

Centered empty state for charts and data panels that have no content.

Exports: `ChartEmptyState`, `ChartEmptyStateProps`

Props (`ChartEmptyStateProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `IconSvgElement` |  | `ChartLineData01Icon` | Hugeicons icon value rendered in the icon tile. |
| `title` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional bold-ish heading rendered above the message. |
| `message` | `string` | yes |  | Required muted body copy that explains why no data is available. |
| `action` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional call-to-action rendered below the message. |
| `size` | `VariantProps<typeof chartEmptyStateVariants>["size"]` |  | `"default"` | Controls the overall visual scale of the empty state. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional Tailwind class names merged onto the root element. |

Variants:
- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `chart-empty-state`

### Empty

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/empty";`

Centred placeholder surface shown when a list or view has no content.

Exports: `Empty`, `EmptyContent`, `EmptyDescription`, `EmptyHeader`, `EmptyMedia`, `EmptyProps`, `EmptyTitle`, `emptyMediaVariants`

Props (`EmptyProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `border` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `true` (the default) the root renders a `border border-dashed` outline, signalling the absence of real content. Pass `false` to suppress the border — useful when the empty state lives inside a card that already provides a boundary. |

Variants:
- `media`: `default`, `icon` (default: `default`)
- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)
- `variant`: `muted`, `info`, `success`, `warning`, `destructive` (default: `muted`)

Data slots: `empty`, `empty-content`, `empty-description`, `empty-header`, `empty-icon`, `empty-title`

### Feature Lock

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/feature-lock";`

Gating overlay that visually locks content behind an upsell prompt.

Exports: `FeatureBadge`, `FeatureBadgeProps`, `FeatureLock`, `FeatureLockProps`

Props (`FeatureBadgeProps`):
Extends: `BadgeProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `plan` | `string` | yes |  | The plan name displayed inside the badge, e.g. `"Pro"` or `"Enterprise"`. |

Props (`FeatureLockProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `locked` | `boolean` | yes |  | When `true` the gating overlay is shown and children are visually obscured. When `false` children are rendered unchanged with no wrapper. |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | The content to gate — rendered normally when unlocked. |
| `title` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Heading shown in the overlay, e.g. `"Upgrade to Pro"`. Accepts any renderable content. |
| `description` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Supporting body copy shown below the title in the overlay. Accepts any renderable content. |
| `planLabel` | `string` |  |  | When provided, a FeatureBadge with this plan name is rendered above the title in the overlay. |
| `action` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | CTA slot — typically an upgrade Button. Rendered below the description inside the overlay. |
| `blur` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `true` (default) a `blur-sm` class is applied to the gated content so it is obscured but still hinted at. Set to `false` to skip blurring (e.g. when the content itself is already a placeholder). |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes applied to the outermost wrapper `<div>`. |

Data slots: `feature-badge`, `feature-lock`

### Notice Banner

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/notice-banner";`

Thin re-export shim — all logic now lives in /banner.

Exports: `Banner`, `BannerActions`, `BannerClose`, `BannerContent`, `BannerDescription`, `BannerIcon`, `BannerProps`, `BannerTitle`, `NoticeBanner`, `NoticeBannerAction`, `NoticeBannerProps`, `NoticeBannerVariants`, `bannerVariants`, `noticeBannerVariants`

Extends: `React.ComponentProps<typeof NoticeBanner>`

- `size`: `xs`, `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `progress`, `progress-indicator`, `progress-label`, `progress-track`, `progress-value`

## Layout

### Accordion

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/accordion";`

A vertically stacked set of collapsible sections, each with a trigger and an animated content panel.

Exports: `Accordion`, `AccordionContent`, `AccordionItem`, `AccordionItemProps`, `AccordionProps`, `AccordionTrigger`, `AccordionTriggerIndicator`, `AccordionTriggerProps`, `AccordionVariant`, `accordionItemVariants`

Props (`AccordionItemProps`):
Extends: `AccordionPrimitive.Item.Props`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `variant` | `AccordionVariant` |  |  | Visual style for this item. Overrides the variant inherited from the parent Accordion when set. |

Props (`AccordionProps`):
Extends: `AccordionPrimitive.Root.Props`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `variant` | `AccordionVariant` |  | `"default"` | Visual style applied to every item. Each AccordionItem can override it locally with its own `variant`. Defaults to `"default"`. |

Props (`AccordionTriggerProps`):
Extends: `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger.Props`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `indicator` | `AccordionTriggerIndicator` |  | `"chevron"` | Built-in collapse/expand indicator placement. - `"chevron"` (default) — down arrow when collapsed, up arrow when open. - `"plus-minus"` — plus glyph when collapsed, minus when open. - `"none"` — suppress the built-in indicator; supply your own as the last child of the trigger (tag it `data-slot="accordion-trigger-icon"` to inherit sizing). |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `outline`, `solid` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `accordion`, `accordion-content`, `accordion-item`, `accordion-trigger`, `accordion-trigger-icon`

### Collapsible

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/collapsible";`

A lightweight single-panel show/hide container driven by a trigger.

Exports: `Collapsible`, `CollapsibleContent`, `CollapsibleProps`, `CollapsibleTrigger`

Props (`CollapsibleProps`):
Extends: `CollapsiblePrimitive.Root.Props`

Data slots: `collapsible`, `collapsible-content`, `collapsible-trigger`

### Frame

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/frame";`

A bordered surface that hosts one or more FramePanel children.

Exports: `Frame`, `FrameDescription`, `FrameFooter`, `FrameHeader`, `FramePanel`, `FrameProps`, `FrameTitle`, `frameVariants`

Props (`FrameProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`, `FrameVariantProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dense` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, FramePanels drop their inner padding. Useful when the panel's content owns its own padding (e.g. a `<DataGrid>`). Default `false`. |
| `stacked` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, sibling FramePanels are joined edge-to-edge with shared borders (no gap, no inner radius between them). Only the first and last panels keep rounded corners. Default `false`. |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `ghost` (default: `default`)
- `spacing`: `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `frame`, `frame-description`, `frame-footer`, `frame-header`, `frame-panel`, `frame-title`

### Resizable

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/resizable";`

Drag handle separator between two ResizablePanel elements.

Exports: `ResizableHandle`, `ResizablePanel`, `ResizablePanelGroup`, `ResizablePanelGroupProps`

Props (`ResizablePanelGroupProps`):
Extends: `ResizablePrimitive.GroupProps`

Data slots: `resizable-handle`, `resizable-panel`, `resizable-panel-group`

### Scroll Area

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/scroll-area";`

A cross-browser custom scroll container with styled overlaid scrollbars.

Exports: `ScrollArea`, `ScrollAreaProps`, `ScrollBar`

Props (`ScrollAreaProps`):
Extends: `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root.Props`

Data slots: `scroll-area`, `scroll-area-scrollbar`, `scroll-area-thumb`, `scroll-area-viewport`

### Separator

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/separator";`

A thin dividing line between sections of content.

Exports: `Separator`, `SeparatorProps`

Props (`SeparatorProps`):
Extends: `SeparatorPrimitive.Props`

Data slots: `separator`

### Shell

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/shell";`

Top-level page layout wrapper that applies consistent padding, gap, and flex-column structure to a page or route section.

Exports: `Shell`, `ShellProps`

Props (`ShellProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"div">`, `VariantProps<typeof shellVariants>`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional CSS classes merged onto the wrapper `<div>`. |
| `variant` | `"default" \| "centered" \| "fluid"` |  |  | Layout preset applied via shellVariants. - `"default"` — full-width with responsive horizontal padding. - `"centered"` — adds `items-center` to horizontally centre children. - `"fluid"` — identical to `"default"` for unrestricted-width layouts. |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `centered`, `fluid` (default: `default`)

Data slots: `shell`

## Patterns

### Calendar RAC

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/calendar-rac";`

Single-date calendar built on React Aria Components.

Exports: `Calendar`, `CalendarProps`, `RangeCalendar`

Props (`CalendarProps`):
Extends: `ComponentProps<typeof CalendarRac>`, `BaseCalendarProps`

Data slots: `calendar`, `range-calendar`

### Calendar With Presets

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/calendar-with-presets";`

A day-picker calendar with a sidebar of quick-select presets.

Exports: `CalendarWithPresets`, `CalendarWithPresetsProps`

Props (`CalendarWithPresetsProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `mode` | `"single" \| "range"` |  | `"single"` | The mode of the calendar. |
| `selected` | `Date \| DateRange \| undefined` |  |  | The selected date or date range. |
| `onSelect` | `\| ((date: Date \| undefined) => void) \| ((range: DateRange \| undefined) => void)` |  |  | Callback fired when the date or date range changes. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | The class name for the root element. |
| `presets` | `CalendarPreset[]` |  |  | Custom presets to show in the sidebar. If not provided, default presets will be used. |
| `disableFutureDates` | `boolean` |  | `true` | Whether to disable future dates. |

Data slots: `calendar-with-presets`

Heavy dependencies: `react-day-picker`

### Card Checkbox

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/card-checkbox";`

Grid wrapper that spaces a collection of CardCheckboxItem components with a consistent gap.

Exports: `CardCheckboxGroup`, `CardCheckboxItem`, `CardCheckboxItemProps`

Props (`CardCheckboxItemProps`):
Extends: `Omit< React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof Checkbox>, "variant" >`, `VariantProps<typeof cardCheckboxVariants>`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Optional icon rendered to the left of the label and description; dimmed via `text-muted-foreground` when the item is disabled. |
| `label` | `React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Primary text for the card; rendered as a `<span>` linked to the checkbox via `aria-labelledby`. |
| `description` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Supporting text rendered below the label; also linked to the checkbox via `aria-describedby` when `id` is provided. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  |  | Disables the Checkbox and applies `cursor-not-allowed` styling to the card via the CVA `disabled` variant. |

Variants:
- `variant`: `default`, `compact` (default: `default`)
- `layout`: `default`, `start` (default: `default`)
- `disabled`: `true`, `false` (default: `false`)

Data slots: `card-checkbox-group`, `card-checkbox-item`

### Checkbox Tree

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/checkbox-tree";`

A headless, renderless checkbox tree that manages tri-state (checked / unchecked / indeterminate) selection across a nested TreeNode hierarchy.

Exports: `CheckboxTree`, `CheckboxTreeProps`

Props (`CheckboxTreeProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `tree` | `TreeNode` | yes |  | Root TreeNode whose entire hierarchy is rendered and managed. |
| `renderNode` | `(props: { node: TreeNode; checked: boolean \| "indeterminate"; onCheckedChange: () => void; children: React.ReactNode; }) => React.ReactNode` | yes |  | Render-prop called for every node in the tree. |

### Credenza

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/credenza";`

Responsive overlay that renders as a centred Dialog on desktop (`>= 768 px`) and as a bottom Drawer on mobile.

Exports: `Credenza`, `CredenzaBody`, `CredenzaClose`, `CredenzaContent`, `CredenzaDescription`, `CredenzaFooter`, `CredenzaHeader`, `CredenzaProps`, `CredenzaTitle`, `CredenzaTrigger`

Props (`CredenzaProps`):
Extends: `BaseProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `open` | `boolean` |  |  | Controlled open state; pair with onOpenChange. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` |  |  | Called when the overlay requests an open/close transition. |

Data slots: `credenza`, `credenza-body`, `credenza-close`, `credenza-content`, `credenza-description`, `credenza-footer`, `credenza-header`, `credenza-title`, `credenza-trigger`

### Date Field RAC

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/datefield-rac";`

An accessible date field that renders each date part (year, month, day) as individually focusable, spinnable segments.

Exports: `DateField`, `DateFieldProps`, `DateInput`, `DateInputProps`, `DateSegment`, `TimeField`, `dateInputStyle`

Props (`DateFieldProps`):
Extends: `DateFieldPropsRac<T>`

Props (`DateInputProps`):
Extends: `DateInputPropsRac`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the React Aria `DateInput` element via `composeRenderProps`; appended after the default dateInputStyle classes (or all classes when `unstyled` is `true`). |
| `unstyled` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, suppresses the default dateInputStyle base classes so the container can be fully re-styled by the caller. |

Data slots: `date-field`, `date-input`, `date-segment`, `time-field`

### Date Input

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/date-input";`

A three-segment MM/DD/YYYY inline date editor with keyboard navigation.

Exports: `DateInput`, `DateInputProps`

Props (`DateInputProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `Date` |  |  | Currently selected date; the component mirrors this into its internal segment state on each render cycle. |
| `onChange` | `(date: Date) => void` | yes |  | Called with a fully valid `Date` whenever the user commits a change; not fired for partial or out-of-range inputs. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the outermost wrapper `<div>` that provides the border, background, and flex layout. |

Data slots: `date-input`

### Date Picker

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/date-picker";`

A single-date picker that reveals a calendar in a popover.

Exports: `DatePicker`, `DatePickerProps`

Props (`DatePickerProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `Date` |  |  | Currently selected date; pass `undefined` to show the placeholder. |
| `onChange` | `(date: Date \| undefined) => void` |  |  | Called with the selected `Date` (or `undefined`) when the user picks a day or clears the selection via the calendar. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  |  | Disables the trigger button, preventing the calendar from opening. |
| `required` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, appends a red asterisk to the label text. |
| `label` | `string` |  |  | Optional `<label>` rendered above the trigger button; linked via `htmlFor` to the button's generated id. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the outermost wrapper `<div>`. |
| `error` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, applies a destructive border to the trigger button. |

Data slots: `date-picker`

### Date Picker With Month Year

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/date-picker-with-month-year";`

A date picker with dropdown selectors for month and year navigation.

Exports: `DatePickerWithMonthYear`, `DatePickerWithMonthYearProps`

Props (`DatePickerWithMonthYearProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `Date` |  |  | The current value (controlled). |
| `onChange` | `(value: Date \| undefined) => void` |  |  | Handler that is called when the value changes. |
| `defaultValue` | `Date` |  |  | The default value (uncontrolled). |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  |  | Whether the date picker is disabled. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional CSS class name for the container. |
| `error` | `boolean` |  |  | Error state for validation styling. |
| `minValue` | `Date` |  |  | Minimum selectable date. |
| `maxValue` | `Date` |  |  | Maximum selectable date. |
| `placeholder` | `string` |  | `"Selecione uma data"` | Placeholder text when no date is selected |

Data slots: `calendar`, `date-picker-content`, `date-picker-trigger`, `date-picker-with-month-year`, `dropdown`, `dropdown-content`, `dropdown-item`, `dropdown-nav`, `dropdown-trigger`

Heavy dependencies: `react-day-picker`

Props (`DateRangePickerProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `value` | `DateRange` |  |  | The selected date range. |
| `onChange` | `(value: DateRange \| undefined) => void` |  |  | Callback fired when the date range changes. |
| `label` | `string` |  |  | The label text for the date range picker. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | The class name for the root element. |
| `error` | `boolean` |  |  | Indicates if the input has an error. |
| `errorMessage` | `string` |  |  | The error message to display when the input has an error. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  |  | Indicates if the input is disabled. |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| `onChange` | `(value: DateRange \| undefined) => void` |  |  | Handler that is called when the value changes. |
| `label` | `string` |  |  | The label for the date range picker. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional CSS class name for the container. |

Heavy dependencies: `react-day-picker`

### ID Cell

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/id-cell";`

Table cell that displays a truncated identifier with a copy-to-clipboard button, intended for use inside a data table column definition.

Exports: `IdCell`, `IdCellProps`

Props (`IdCellProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the outermost wrapper `div`. |
| `id` | `string` | yes |  | Full identifier string to display and copy. |
| `length` | `number` |  | `6` | Number of characters to show before the trailing ellipsis. |

Data slots: `id-cell`

Heavy dependencies: `motion`

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

- `size`: `sm`, `default`, `lg` (default: `default`)

### Input Password with Strength Indicator

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/input-password-with-strength-indicator";`

A password field that reveals a live strength meter and per-requirement checklist after the user first focuses the input.

Exports: `InputPasswordWithStrengthIndicator`, `InputPasswordWithStrengthIndicatorProps`

Props (`InputPasswordWithStrengthIndicatorProps`):
Extends: `Omit< React.ComponentProps<"input">, "type" >`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | `string` |  |  | Optional `<Label>` rendered above the input; linked via `htmlFor={id}`. Requires `id` to be set on the component for the association to work. |
| `error` | `boolean` |  |  | When `true`, applies a destructive border and forces `aria-invalid` regardless of the computed strength score. |
| `showStrengthIndicator` | `boolean` |  | `true` | When `false`, hides the strength progress bar and requirements checklist, rendering the field as a plain show/hide password input. |

Data slots: `input`, `input-container`, `input-password-with-strength-indicator`, `label`, `strength-indicator`, `strength-progress`, `strength-progress-bar`, `strength-requirement`, `strength-requirement-text`, `strength-requirements`, `strength-requirements-list`, `strength-text`, `visibility-toggle`

### Input with Addons

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/input-with-addons";`

A text input flanked by optional muted addon panels for contextual prefix or suffix labels (e.g. protocol, domain extension, or unit).

Exports: `InputWithAddons`, `InputWithAddonsProps`

Props (`InputWithAddonsProps`):
Extends: `React.ComponentProps<"input">`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `leading` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Content rendered in the muted panel before the input (e.g. `"https://"` or a currency symbol). Omit to suppress the leading addon. |
| `trailing` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Content rendered in the muted panel after the input (e.g. `".com"` or a unit label). Omit to suppress the trailing addon. |
| `containerClassName` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the outer bordered wrapper `<div>`. |

Data slots: `input`, `input-with-addons`, `leading-addon`, `trailing-addon`

### Input with Inline Button

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/input-with-inline-button";`

A text input with an attached button fused to its trailing edge, suitable for search bars, coupon fields, and newsletter sign-ups.

Exports: `InputWithInlineButton`, `InputWithInlineButtonProps`

Props (`InputWithInlineButtonProps`):
Extends: `InputProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `button` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Custom element rendered instead of the built-in Button. |
| `buttonText` | `string` |  |  | Label text rendered inside the built-in Button. |
| `onButtonClick` | `() => void` |  |  | Click handler forwarded to the built-in Button. |
| `buttonClassName` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes merged onto the button or its container element. |
| `buttonAriaLabel` | `string` |  |  | `aria-label` for the built-in Button when `buttonText` is insufficient. |
| `wrapperClassName` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes merged onto the outer flex wrapper `<div>`. |
| `buttonType` | `"button" \| "submit" \| "reset"` |  | `"submit"` | HTML `type` attribute of the built-in Button. |

Data slots: `button`, `button-container`, `input`, `input-with-inline-button`

### Input with Inner Tags

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/input-with-inner-tags";`

A tag-input field that renders inline Tag badges inside the input area, letting users build a list of values within a single control.

Exports: `InputWithInnerTags`, `InputWithInnerTagsProps`, `Tag`

Props (`InputWithInnerTagsProps`):

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `string` |  |  | `id` forwarded to the inner `<Input>` for external `<label htmlFor>`. |
| `placeholder` | `string` |  | `"Add a tag"` | Placeholder text shown in the inner `<Input>` when no value is typed. |
| `tags` | `Tag[]` | yes |  | Controlled list of current tags; update via onTagsChange. |
| `onTagsChange` | `(newTags: Tag[]) => void` | yes |  | Called with the full updated tag list after every add or remove action. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` |  | `false` | Disables the inner `<Input>` and all remove buttons. |
| `className` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes merged onto the inner `<Input>` element. |
| `containerClassName` | `string` |  |  | Extra classes merged onto the outermost container `<div>`. |

Data slots: `input-with-inner-tags`

### Input with Loader

Import: `import { ... } from "@strait/ui/components/input-with-loader";`

A text input that swaps its leading icon for an animated loading spinner while an async operation is in progress.

Exports: `InputWithLoader`, `InputWithLoaderProps`

Props (`InputWithLoaderProps`):
Extends: `InputProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `loading` | `boolean` |  | `false` | When `true`, replaces the icon slot with an animated spinner. |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Decorative node rendered at the leading edge; replaced by the spinner while `isLoading` is `true`. |
| `endIcon` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Node rendered as a clickable button at the trailing edge of the input (e.g. a clear or submit icon). Requires endIconAriaLabel. |
| `onEndIconClick` | `() => void` |  |  | Click handler forwarded to the trailing-edge button wrapping endIcon. |
| `endIconAriaLabel` | `string` |  |  | Accessible label for the trailing-edge endIcon button; required by screen readers when using an icon-only affordance. |

Data slots: `end-icon`, `input`, `input-with-loader`, `start-icon`

Props (`InputWithStartIconProps`):
Extends: `InputProps`

| Prop | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` |  |  | Decorative node overlaid at the leading edge of the input; rendered `pointer-events-none` so it never intercepts clicks or focus. |
| `label` | `string` |  |  | Optional `<label>` text rendered above the input and linked via `htmlFor={props.id}`; requires `id` to be set for the association. |
| `containerClassName` | `string` |  |  | Additional classes merged onto the relative wrapper `<div>` that contains the input and the icon overlay. |
